title: Oracle's 40% surge has sent support to over 425 ETFs on the Street
content:
[Oracle headquarter building in Bucharest. Logo of the Oracle company on a office building.]
Cristi Croitoru/iStock Editorial via Getty Images

Shares of Oracle (NYSE:ORCL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ORCL]) climbed sharply on Wednesday's trading session, extending momentum from the company’s latest earnings release that sent the stock surging over 40%.

The strong results have not only bolstered confidence in the Texas-based software giant but also triggered notable spillover effects across the exchange traded fund market. 

Oracle currently sits in the portfolios of 432 ETFs, with combined ownership exceeding 255 million shares. This level of institutional representation underscores Oracle’s role as a cornerstone holding in many of Wall Street’s most widely traded funds.

As a result, the company’s earnings-driven rally is reverberating well beyond its own stock, lifting broader fund performance and drawing fresh attention from investors who track specific ETFs.

Below are the 10 ETFs with the heaviest exposure to Oracle, highlighting the funds most directly benefiting from the tech leader’s post-earnings momentum.

No. 1: iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (BATS:IGV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IGV]), 9.91% allocation.

No. 2: Pacer Data and Digital Revolution ETF (NYSEARCA:TRFK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TRFK]), 9.18% allocation.

No. 3: First Trust NASDAQ Technology Dividend Index Fund (NASDAQ:TDIV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TDIV]), 8.97% allocation.

No. 4: Janus Henderson Transformational Growth ETF (BATS:JXX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JXX]), 8.92% allocation.

No. 5: FT Vest Technology Dividend Target Income ETF (TDIVI), 8.81% allocation.

No. 6: iShares Texas Equity ETF (NASDAQ:TEXN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TEXN]), 8.62% allocation.

No. 7: Polen Capital Global Growth ETF (NYSEARCA:PCGG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PCGG]), 6.43% allocation.

No. 8: VistaShares Target 15 USA Quality Income ETF (NYSEARCA:QUSA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QUSA]), 6.41% allocation.

No. 9: SGI Enhanced Market Leaders ETF (NASDAQ:LDRX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LDRX]), 6.08% allocation.

No. 10: Scharf Global Opportunity ETF (NASDAQ:GKAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GKAT]), 5.99% allocation.
